name: Marble Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 3 packages Cream Cheese (8 oz each), softened
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare Chocolate Crumb Crust.
2. Preheat oven to 450°F (232°C).
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat Cream Cheese with 3/4 cup Sugar, Sour Cream, and 2 teaspoons Vanilla until smooth.
4. Gradually add Flour, stirring well.
5. Add Eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition; set aside.
6. Stir Cocoa Powder and remaining 1/4 cup Sugar together.
7. Mix in Oil, remaining 1/2 teaspoon Vanilla, and stir into the Cream Cheese mixture.
8. Spoon alternating layers of plain and chocolate batters over the prepared Chocolate Crumb Crust, finishing with chocolate on top.
9. Gently swirl the batter with a spatula or knife for a marbled effect.
10. Bake at 450°F (232°C) for 10 minutes.
11. Reduce oven temperature to 250°F (121°C). Continue baking for 30 minutes.
12. Turn off the oven; leave the cheesecake in the oven for an additional 30 minutes without opening the door.
13. Remove from the oven and let cool to room temperature.
14. Refrigerate several hours or overnight, then remove from the pan.
15. Cover and refrigerate leftover cheesecake.
9-10 servings
Chocolate Crumb Crust:
1-1/4 cups Vanilla Wafers (about 40)
1/3 cup Powdered Sugar
1/3 cup Cocoa Powder
1/4 cup melted Butter or Margarine
Press mixture into the bottom and up the sides of a 9-inch Springform Pan. Bake for 8 minutes, then cool completely.
